tooling_milestone.role: Starts from a large object root field load, follows local register aliases through selected child-pointer offsets, and reports accesses to small child fields that are too common for global displacement scans.
tooling_milestone.why_added: Global scans for child offsets such as +0x38 returned thousands of unrelated hits. The new tracker makes those offsets usable by requiring a proven local path from the client controller root.

root_hit.meaning: Loads the active controller from client +0x198D0.

interpretation.positive_result: The context-aware trace independently rediscovers the known OTL consumer and proves that the noisy global +0x38 scan can be made tractable when rooted at client +0x198D0.
interpretation.negative_result: Across the 46 functions with non-stack client +0x198D0 root references, the simple local path tracker found no writes to controller child +0x110 +0x38. The events are all reads/address-takes in the OTL URL consumer.
interpretation.bounded_inference: The one-shot OTL token producer probably does not write through the same local pattern of loading client +0x198D0 and immediately following child +0x110 in one function. It may receive the child pointer directly, use a copied controller pointer across a call boundary, write through a helper, or target a sibling/state object that is later installed as child +0x110.

opennxt_relevance.server_meaning: This continues to map the desktop auth/web OTL handoff path, not the main game-login socket. The server-side value is avoiding protocol confusion while identifying any native client auth/session prerequisites around launcher or Steam mode.
opennxt_relevance.practical_value: The next OpenNXT-facing target is still the producer of the one-shot OTL token or the service dispatch that consumes the formatted OTL URL, because that determines whether a private server must emulate this web-auth path or can bypass it for direct login flows.

next_targets: Extend pe-object-field-trace to accept explicit function windows or seed registers so helper functions that receive the controller child pointer directly can be traced.
Use callsite register recovery around calls in 0x1401F6D51..0x1401F6E30 and surrounding auth handlers to identify helpers that populate or clear child +0x38.
Classify the formatter call at 0x1401F6E58 and service dispatch through client +0x536B8 to determine how the formatted OTL URL leaves the client.
